title: Dynamic Lane Management
description:
You are managing a lane where people stand in order. Initially, N people are present in the lane.
You are then given M operations. Each operation is either insertion (I position value) or deletion (D position) at a specified position.
Process all operations efficiently and output the final order.
inputFormat:
First line: N
Second line: N integers
Third line: M
Next M lines: operation type, position, and value (for insertion)
explanation: Use a Python list to simulate insert and delete at positions.
starterCode:
n = int(input())
lane = list(map(int, input().split()))
m = int(input())
for _ in range(m):
    op = input().split()
    # 'I pos val' = insert, 'D pos' = delete
    # Write your logic here
print(*lane)

title: Kth Largest Without Sorting

Given an array of integers and an integer K, return the Kth largest element.
You cannot sort the array. Use a heap-based approach for O(n log k) time.
inputFormat:

Second line: n integers
Third line: k
outputFormat: Single integer — the Kth largest element
explanation: Maintain a min-heap of size K. The root will be the Kth largest.

n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
k = int(input())
# Write your heap logic here

title: Design Twitter Feed
description:
Implement Twitter: postTweet(userId, tweetId), getNewsFeed(userId), follow(u, v), unfollow(u, v).
getNewsFeed returns 10 most recent tweets from user and followees.
inputFormat:
First line: Q
Next Q lines: POST uid tid, FEED uid, FOLLOW u v, UNFOLLOW u v
outputFormat: For each FEED, print up to 10 tweet ids space-separated
explanation: Track tweets with timestamps. On FEED, collect from user+followees, sort by time.
starterCode:
from collections import defaultdict
import heapq
q = int(input())
tweets = defaultdict(list)  # uid -> [(time, tid)]
following = defaultdict(set)  # uid -> set of followees
time = 0
# Process operations

explanation: Catalan(N) = C(2N,N)/(N+1). Use modular arithmetic.

title: Access Value by Key
description: Given a dictionary and a key, print the value. If key doesn't exist, print 'Key not found'.
inputFormat:
First line: n (pairs)
Next n lines: key value
Last line: search key
explanation: Use dict.get() with a default value.
starterCode:
n = int(input())
d = {}
for _ in range(n):
    k, v = input().split()
    d[k] = v
key = input()
# Print value or 'Key not found'

testCases.input:
3
name Alice
age 25
city Delhi
name

title: Delete Key Value
description: Given a dictionary and a key to delete, remove the key-value pair and print remaining pairs.
inputFormat:
First line: n
Next n lines: key value
Last line: key to delete
outputFormat: Remaining pairs as 'key: value' one per line, sorted by key
explanation: Use del dict[key] or dict.pop(key). Print sorted items.
starterCode:
n = int(input())
d = {}
for _ in range(n):
    k, v = input().split()
    d[k] = v
key = input()
# Delete and print

testCases.input:
3
name Alice
age 25
city Delhi
age

title: Merge Two Dictionaries
description: Merge two dictionaries. If same key exists in both, keep the value from the second dict.
inputFormat:
First line: n1
Next n1 lines: key value
Next line: n2
Next n2 lines: key value
outputFormat: Merged dict as 'key: value' sorted by key
explanation: Use {**dict1, **dict2} or dict1.update(dict2).

# Use dict comprehension

title: Sum and Average
description: Calculate the sum and average of an array. Print average to 2 decimal places.
inputFormat:
First line: n
Second line: n integers
explanation: Sum all elements. Average = sum / n.

title: Search Element
description: Search for a target element in an array. Print its index (0-based) or -1 if not found.
inputFormat:
First line: n
Second line: n integers
Third line: target
explanation: Linear search through array.

title: Separate Odd Even
description: Separate odd and even integers from an array. Print all even numbers first, then odd numbers.
inputFormat:
First line: n
Second line: n integers
outputFormat:
Even: e1 e2 ...
Odd: o1 o2 ...
explanation: Filter evens and odds separately, print each group.

title: Matrix Addition
description: Add two N×M matrices and print the result.
inputFormat:
First line: n m
Next n lines: row of matrix A
Next n lines: row of matrix B
outputFormat: Result matrix, each row on new line

title: Factorial Recursion
description: Find factorial of N using recursion.
inputFormat: Single integer N
explanation: factorial(n) = n * factorial(n-1), base case factorial(0) = 1

title: Fibonacci Recursion
description: Find the Nth Fibonacci number using recursion. F(0)=0, F(1)=1.
inputFormat: Single integer N
explanation: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2)

title: Power Recursion
description: Calculate base^exp using recursion.
inputFormat: Two integers: base exp
explanation: power(b, e) = b * power(b, e-1), base case power(b, 0) = 1

title: GCD using Euclid
description: Find GCD of two numbers using Euclid's recursive algorithm.
inputFormat: Two integers a b
explanation: gcd(a, b) = gcd(b, a%b), base case gcd(a, 0) = a

title: Tower of Hanoi
description: Print all moves to solve Tower of Hanoi with N disks. Move from rod A to C using B as auxiliary.
inputFormat: Single integer N
outputFormat: Each move as: Move disk d from X to Y
explanation: hanoi(n, from, to, aux): move n-1 to aux, move disk n to target, move n-1 from aux to target

testCases.output:
Move disk 1 from A to B
Move disk 2 from A to C
Move disk 1 from B to C
Move disk 1 from A to C
